title: Real Robot vs Super Robot
wikitext:
[]: Real Robot vs Super Robot             Quote Post by V » Fri Oct 31, 2008 2:38 pm someone new to Eva asked me if it was categorized as Real Robot, or Super Robot Real Robot is realistic stuff, like the more realistic Gundam things; battlemechs that are real military hardware, essentially a walking tank. They've got different production models like cars and tanks do, and they seem like just a future advancement of military technology Super Robots are things like Mazinger Z or The Big O or Voltron: the physical dimensions and capabilities vary greatly or they never even try to define them, it can come up with new abilities if you need it that really defy actual physics, and they have ***super long powerup sequences for super-attacks (also, Power Rangers) The best way I ultimately chose to phrase it is that " Evangelions are a very realistic take on Super Robots, mixing in some elements of Real Robot, while still leaning strongly towards Super Robot " That is, they have no long powerup for special attack sequences, and their attacks, far from being magical or swords, are realistic and savage: take the Sachiel fight in episode 2, there is not super-powerup sequence to "berserker mode"; it just runs amok, and just savagely beats the Angel with its bare hands. That's more "Real Robot" as is referring to them by production number and model number: "Prototype Unit-00", "Test-Type Unit-01", "Production Model Unit-02", "Mass Production Models" etc. That's destinctly Real Robot. Overall i think its part of Eva's general story flavor, of inserting extreme (almost self-satirical criticsm) realism into what had up to that time been a pretty unrealistic genre. Real Robot pilots tend to be happy teenagers who are super-gifted. Eva turned that on its head by having a main cast with serious psychological issues. Similarly, they put a VERY realistic twist on Super Robot, with the emphasis on the technology and mechanics of the inside of Nerv HQ etc. still, Evangelions (and Angels) are fundamentally "Humans playing with the fire of the Gods" and we're dealing with stuff we cannot understand. In the later stages, yes, Evas start wielding their AT Field as a weapon and such (Asuka vs JSSDF) but that's basically just a forcefield or telekinesis (Eva-01 vs Zeruel) ; it's still not even near the super-powerup-sequences seen in PowerRangers or Mazinger Z type stuff. So instead of describing it as "a mix of Super Robot and Real Robot", I think the best way to describe it would be more "it's a Super Robot, but with a very realistic "Real Robot" style twist"

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Fri Oct 31, 2008 2:43 pm I think that the point is moot: the robots are not the main focus of the story, merely plot devices. Really, Eva firmly belongs in the psychological genres of stories, with sci-fi elements mixed in. Besides, Eva's aren't robots in the first place. They're unobtanium biotech.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Oct 31, 2008 3:07 pm Formless One wrote: the robots are not the main focus of the story, merely plot devices.

[]: Re: Real Robot vs Super Robot             Quote Post by SeanTucker » Fri Oct 31, 2008 5:06 pm V wrote: Real Robot pilots tend to be happy teenagers who are super-gifted. Eva turned that on its head by having a main cast with serious psychological issues.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Nov 01, 2008 12:56 am A better way to mindscrew your friend: "Evangelion is a robot show that doesn't even HAVE any robots, and it's not about them anyway."

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sat Nov 01, 2008 1:18 am Real Robot is realistic stuff

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sat Nov 01, 2008 1:34 am Ok, I'm going to be an ass and point out that from a strictly realistic point of view, mecha of any sort are impractical and unrealistic, even western style mechs like Battletech and the Imperial walkers of Star Wars. Its a maintenance and armor thing. That and the superiority of aircraft. Course, that doesn't keep people from treating them like a real weapon and not Supermans seventy foot tall uncle. Anyway...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Nov 01, 2008 2:55 am Realism would be limited to things like powered infantry suits. I'm talking something along the lines of those featured in Starship Troopers (the book), WH40K's Terminators, Jin Roh, or a massively nerfed Iron Man. Once you go from wearing it to piloting it realism is a pretense at best.

[]:             Quote Post by rberlin » Mon Nov 03, 2008 9:50 am AuraTwilight wrote: A better way to mindscrew your friend: "Evangelion is a robot show that doesn't even HAVE any robots, and it's not about them anyway."

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Mon Nov 03, 2008 2:23 pm Wikipedia, 'Super Robot' wrote: Inevitably, there are some types of mecha that are difficult to classify as either a real robot or a super robot. Some of these include the Aura Battlers from Aura Battler Dunbine or the Evangelion units from Neon Genesis Evangelion, which follow the general motif of real robots, but their origin and abilities are more like the typical super robot. The Mortar Headds from Five Star Stories are unique artifacts, treated like individual works of art by the fictional society present in the story, and their power often borderlines on super robot. However, their intricate engineering and the motif of their weaponry is often scientifically explained by series creator Mamoru Nagano which makes them very real robot-esque in other ways. Mecha which employ both Super Robot and Real Robot principles are referred to as Hybrid Robots; since the production of Evangelion, this approach has gained some popularity and developed into its own niche, as evidenced by shows such as Brain Powerd, RahXephon, Overman King Gainer and Zegapain. Nevertheless, several pure Super Robot series have been produced in modern times, such as GaoGaiGar, Gravion and Godannar. The 2007 anime Tengen Toppa Gurren Lagann is notable for featuring 1970s-inspired Super Robot protagonists (Spirals) in conflict with Evangelion-inspired Hybrid Robot antagonists (Anti-Spirals) in the second half of the series.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Nov 03, 2008 4:13 pm rberlin wrote: AuraTwilight wrote: A better way to mindscrew your friend: "Evangelion is a robot show that doesn't even HAVE any robots, and it's not about them anyway."

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Nov 03, 2008 5:42 pm Everything in Eva's a metaphor for cocks.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Mon Nov 03, 2008 6:56 pm master_lloyd wrote: Everything in Eva's a metaphor for cocks.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Mon Nov 03, 2008 7:03 pm that and it was slow as hell, although after a long acceleration it walks pretty fast

[]:             Quote Post by Xavierla » Sun Dec 02, 2012 10:58 pm Evangelion is a cross sub-genre in between the super robot sub-genre and the real robot sub-genre. It falls under the sub-genre of super real robot, where super robots are used by a military fraction for some hidden agenda. Gunbuster, like Evangelion falls under a cross sub-genre in between the super and real robot sub-genres but it falls under a different one. Gunbuster falls under the cross sub-genre of real super robot, where a unique robot with fantastic powers is used by the military as a super weapon. Now there are even animes that go further than that like this great anime called NYE. It involves an entire military fraction of mysterious origins using many super robots as a super weapon while also keeping a hidden agenda for the robots.
summary: []: Real Robot vs Super Robot             Quote Post by V » Fri Oct 31, 2008 2:38 pm someone new to Eva asked me if it was categorized as Real Robot, or Super Robot Real Robot is realistic stuff, like the more realistic Gundam things; battlemechs that are real military hardware, essentially a walking tank. They've got different production models like cars and tanks do, and they seem like just a future advancement of military technology
